message:
feat(engine): expand 8 core modules with canonical encoding, compiler policy, conformance catalog, and more

Massive implementation expansion across 8 engine modules, totaling ~4,800
lines of new functionality. These modules form the infrastructure layer
for deterministic compilation, conformance verification, and runtime
type-safe encoding within the FrankenEngine.

Expanded modules:

- canonical_encoding.rs (+722): Deterministic binary encoding/decoding for
  engine values, ensuring identical byte representations across platforms
  for hash stability and cross-node verification. Implements canonical
  ordering of map keys and normalization of floating-point edge cases.

- compiler_policy.rs (+755): Compile-time policy enforcement framework that
  gates IR emission based on configurable security policies. Policies can
  restrict instruction patterns, enforce call-depth limits, and require
  capability annotations on host-call sites before codegen proceeds.

- conformance_catalog.rs (+937): Comprehensive catalog of conformance test
  vectors mapped to specification sections. Provides structured test
  discovery, filtering by spec section, and pass/fail tracking with
  evidence linkage for release gate decisions.

- engine_object_id.rs (+566): Type-safe object identity system replacing
  raw integer handles. Implements generation-counted IDs with GC epoch
  validation to detect use-after-free of engine objects at the type level.

- flow_envelope.rs (+805): Envelope wrapping for inter-module data flow,
  carrying provenance metadata (source module, trust level, schema version)
  alongside payloads. Enables audit trails and trust-boundary enforcement
  without modifying payload types.

- marker_stream.rs (+518): Append-only stream of typed markers emitted
  during execution for deterministic replay and post-hoc analysis.
  Supports checkpoint/restore and provides iterator-based consumption
  for offline analysis tooling.

- opportunity_matrix.rs (+141): Additional opportunity cost analysis
  entries extending the existing matrix with new decision categories
  for compiler optimization trade-offs.

- test_logging_schema.rs (+348): Expanded structured logging schema for
  test infrastructure, adding new event types for conformance gates,
  benchmark comparisons, and evidence collection with stable field names.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
